title: Power Integrations, Inc. (NASDAQ:POWI) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Power Integrations reported Q1 2026 revenue of $108.3 million, up 3% year-over-year, with non-GAAP earnings of $0.25 per diluted share. Industrial segment performance remained strong, growing 23% annually. CEO Jennifer Lloyd emphasized three strategic priorities: improving customer alignment in product development, streamlining the product pipeline for faster time-to-market, and driving operational efficiency. The company announced hiring Mike Balow as SVP of Worldwide Sales, bringing extensive experience from onsemi, Infineon, and Cypress. The company is pursuing growth in three major areas. In automotive, Power Integrations is currently in production or in design engagements with 17 of the top 20 EV manufacturers and targeting $100 per vehicle in addressable content long-term. For data center applications, the company estimates its serviceable addressable market will exceed $1 billion by 2030, focusing on auxiliary power supplies, solid-state transformers, and high-voltage GaN technology. New products include TinySwitch-5 and TOPSwitchGaN, which can double the power capability of the TOPSwitch architecture to 440 watts. For Q2 2026, management projects revenue between $115-120 million with non-GAAP gross margin of 54-55%.
